desc: Bangkok is less a single city break than a high-energy operating system for Thailand: royal temples and river ferries in Rattanakosin, food alleys in Chinatown, malls and BTS links in Sukhumvit/Siam, weekend-market sprawl at Chatuchak, and two airports feeding beach, island and northern itineraries. It rewards planning around heat, rain, traffic and airport choice, but it is one of Asia's strongest food-first stopovers when you build in air-conditioned pauses.
hls: Markets from Chatuchak to Talat Noi

todo.name: Grand Palace and Wat Phra Kaew
todo.desc: Go early, dress correctly and pair the palace complex with nearby Wat Pho.

todo.name: Chao Phraya ferry day
todo.desc: Use public boats to link temples, old quarters and newer riverside malls without sitting in traffic.

todo.name: Chinatown food crawl
todo.desc: Build an evening around Yaowarat snacks, seafood and neon-lit side streets.

todo.name: Chatuchak Market
todo.desc: Visit on the weekend for the widest market choice, allowing heat breaks and luggage space.

todo.name: Wat Pho and massage-school area
todo.desc: Pair the Reclining Buddha with a traditional massage nearby, keeping clothing and shoe etiquette in mind.

todo.name: Talat Noi and river warehouses
todo.desc: Walk the lanes between old shophouses, shrines, street art and coffee stops for a slower counterpoint to malls and rooftop bars.

wifi.note: Excellent in hotels, serviced apartments, coworking cafes and malls; buy an eSIM/local SIM for taxis, food courts, markets and two-airport transfers.

prac.fltNote: Bangkok has realistic London nonstop service into Suvarnabhumi, including LGW-BKK flights in the current long-haul schedule as well as Heathrow options; LCY is not practical. Don Mueang is mainly useful for low-cost regional hops. Fares are usually most pressured around December-January winter sun, Easter/Songkran, July-August UK school holidays and Thai holiday peaks.
prac.lang: Thai; English is common in main tourism areas
prac.visa: British citizens can normally visit Thailand visa-exempt for 60 days for tourism/business, with possible extension; complete Thailand's digital arrival card before arrival and verify passport validity.
alerts: Heat and humidity are draining year-round, and April is especially punishing despite Songkran demand.
Temple dress codes are enforced at the Grand Palace and Wat Phra Kaew; cover shoulders and legs before arriving.
Traffic can make airport transfers much longer than distance suggests; the Airport Rail Link is often safer for Suvarnabhumi at rush hour.
Do not mix up BKK and DMK on onward flights; allow generous time if transferring between airports.

sweet: December to February is the most comfortable Bangkok window, with lower rain and slightly less oppressive humidity; November is a good value shoulder month, while April is culturally lively but very hot.
